home.jsonLdDescription: Free online tool to convert any file to Markdown
home.definition: tomdnow is a free web tool that converts 20+ file formats — including PDF, Word, Excel, PowerPoint, HWP, YouTube, and more — to clean Markdown. Files are processed entirely in memory and never stored on servers. No sign-up required.

pdf.title: PDF to Markdown — tomdnow
pdf.subtitle: Transform your PDF documents into clean, readable Markdown files.
pdf.seoDescription: Free online PDF to Markdown converter. Upload your PDF and get clean Markdown instantly.
pdf.seoKeywords: pdf to markdown, convert pdf to md, pdf markdown converter
pdf.howTo1Desc: Drag and drop or click to select your PDF file.
pdf.howTo2Desc: Our tool converts the PDF to clean Markdown automatically.
pdf.howTo3Desc: Preview the result and download your .md file.
pdf.why1: Edit PDF content in any text editor
pdf.why2: Use in documentation systems like GitHub, Notion, or Obsidian
pdf.why3: Feed into AI/LLM tools for analysis
pdf.why4: Version control your documents with Git
pdf.faq1A: Yes, completely free with no sign-up required.
pdf.faq2Q: What is the maximum file size?
pdf.faq2A: You can upload PDF files up to 10MB.
pdf.faq3A: We preserve headings, lists, tables, and links. Complex layouts may be simplified for readability.
pdf.faq4Q: Is my file stored on the server?
pdf.faq4A: No. Your file is processed in memory and immediately deleted after conversion.
pdf.definitionParagraph: tomdnow's PDF to Markdown converter transforms PDF documents into clean, structured Markdown files. It preserves headings, lists, tables, and links while simplifying complex layouts for maximum readability. Files are processed entirely in memory and never stored on servers.
pdf.detailParagraph: Powered by Microsoft's open-source MarkItDown engine, the converter handles multi-page PDFs with mixed content including text, tables, headers, and embedded lists. Output is compatible with GitHub, Obsidian, Notion, and any Markdown-based editor or documentation system. The tool supports files up to 10MB and completes most conversions in under 5 seconds.

docx.faq4A: No. Your file is processed in memory and immediately deleted after conversion.
docx.definitionParagraph: tomdnow's Word to Markdown converter transforms Microsoft Word documents (.docx) into clean, structured Markdown. It preserves document hierarchy including headings, bold/italic formatting, lists, and tables while stripping proprietary Word formatting.
docx.detailParagraph: The converter uses Microsoft's MarkItDown engine to parse the OOXML structure of DOCX files, extracting text content with semantic structure intact. This makes it ideal for migrating documentation from Word to Markdown-based platforms like GitHub wikis, Obsidian vaults, or static site generators like Jekyll and Hugo.

txt.definitionParagraph: tomdnow's TXT to Markdown converter wraps plain text files in proper Markdown formatting. While text files are already readable, the converter adds structure by detecting potential headings, lists, and paragraphs.
txt.detailParagraph: This tool is useful for importing legacy text files, README files, and plain text notes into Markdown-based systems like Obsidian or Notion. The converter preserves the original text content while adding Markdown syntax for better structure and readability.

rtf.definitionParagraph: tomdnow's RTF to Markdown converter transforms Rich Text Format documents into clean Markdown. Bold, italic, headings, and list formatting are preserved while RTF-specific styling is stripped.
rtf.detailParagraph: RTF is a legacy document format supported by virtually all word processors. The converter is useful for migrating old documents, TextEdit files, and WordPad output into modern Markdown-based workflows. Text content and basic formatting are preserved while proprietary RTF commands are removed.

about.title: About tomdnow
about.metaDescription: Learn about tomdnow, the free file-to-Markdown converter built by Park Gamsa. Privacy-first, open-source powered.
about.heroSubtitle: A free, privacy-first tool to convert any file to Markdown
about.storyP1: tomdnow was created by Park Gamsa, a developer based in South Korea, to solve a simple but persistent problem: converting files to Markdown shouldn't require sign-ups, subscriptions, or trusting a third party with your data.
about.storyP2: What started as a personal tool for converting Korean government documents (HWP/HWPX) into Markdown for AI processing quickly grew into a full-featured converter supporting 20+ file formats.
about.storyP3: Today, tomdnow processes thousands of conversions daily across 8 languages, serving developers, researchers, students, and knowledge workers who need their documents in Markdown for tools like Obsidian, Notion, GitHub, and AI systems like ChatGPT and Claude.
about.techP1: tomdnow is powered by Microsoft's open-source MarkItDown engine, which means your conversions benefit from enterprise-grade parsing without the enterprise price tag.
about.techP2: Every file is processed entirely in memory. Nothing is written to disk, nothing is stored in a database, and nothing persists after your browser tab closes. The conversion happens, you get your Markdown, and the data is gone.
about.techP3: The tool supports 20+ file formats including office documents (PDF, DOCX, XLSX, PPTX), Korean documents (HWP, HWPX), data formats (CSV, JSON, XML), ebooks (EPUB), email archives (MSG), image OCR, YouTube transcripts, and ZIP archives containing any supported format.
about.privacyP1: We believe privacy isn't a feature — it's a foundation. tomdnow was built from day one with a zero-storage architecture. Your files never touch a permanent storage layer.
about.privacyP2: All transfers are encrypted with TLS 1.3, and the conversion backend runs in isolated containers that are recycled regularly.
about.privacyP3: We deliberately chose not to implement user accounts, analytics cookies, or file upload logging. The only data we collect is anonymous usage metrics through privacy-respecting analytics. Your documents remain yours — always.
about.missionP1: Markdown is the universal language of documentation, note-taking, and AI. We want to make it effortless to get any document into Markdown — whether you're a developer, researcher, student, or office worker.
about.missionP2: tomdnow will always have a free tier. We believe essential document conversion should be accessible to everyone.
about.founderBio: Software developer based in South Korea with expertise in web applications, document processing, and developer tools. Park Gamsa built tomdnow after repeatedly encountering the frustration of converting Korean government documents (HWP format) for use in modern tools. What started as a weekend project evolved into a mission to make document conversion free, private, and accessible to everyone regardless of their technical skill level.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.dataCollectionText: tomdnow does not collect personal data. We do not require sign-up or login.
privacy.fileProcessingText: Uploaded files are processed in memory and immediately deleted. We do not store your files.
privacy.analyticsText: We may use anonymous analytics to improve the service. No personally identifiable information is collected.
privacy.contactText: For questions, please reach out via our GitHub repository.

terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.serviceText: tomdnow provides free file-to-Markdown conversion. The service is provided "as is" without warranty.
terms.usageText: You may use tomdnow for personal and commercial purposes. Do not upload illegal or copyrighted content without authorization.
terms.limitationsText: We reserve the right to limit usage to prevent abuse. File size is limited to 10MB per conversion.

security.title: Security — tomdnow
security.subtitle: tomdnow is built from the ground up to ensure your files are never stored, never shared, and never at risk.
security.memoryProcessingDesc: Your files are processed entirely in memory. Nothing is ever written to disk on our servers.
security.instantDeletionDesc: The moment your conversion is complete, all file data is immediately purged from memory.
security.tlsEncryptionDesc: All data transmitted between your browser and our servers is protected with TLS encryption.
security.noAccountDesc: We never ask you to sign up or log in. No personal information is collected, ever.
security.openSourceDesc: Our conversion engine is open source. You can inspect the code and verify our security claims yourself.
security.comparisonDesc: See how tomdnow stacks up against other file conversion services when it comes to privacy and security.
security.faq1Q: Is my file stored on your servers?
security.faq1A: No. All files are processed entirely in memory and never written to disk. Data is discarded immediately after conversion.
security.faq2A: Yes. All data transfers use TLS 1.3 encryption. Your files are protected in transit between your browser and our servers.
security.faq3Q: Can tomdnow employees see my files?
security.faq3A: No. Files exist only in memory during the conversion process. There is no persistent storage, logging, or access mechanism for uploaded content.
security.faq4A: tomdnow is powered by Microsoft's open-source MarkItDown engine. Our conversion backend uses this well-audited library for document processing.
